# Issue #69: Visualize dispersion

## Question

Visualize dispersion

I love how the Visa Chart Components incorporate best practices for accessibility, e.g. always provide a table representation of the data. But as soon as the components are used for inference rather than description, VCC lacks a good way to visualize the dispersion of data, e.g. via confidence intervals around point estimates. This could be implemented as error bars around bar charts or bands/ribbons around line charts.

Providing information about the variance / standard error besides a variable's mean is considered best practice in the communication of statistical results and I would love to see that implemented for VCC.

## Answer

Hi @fkohrt,

Thanks so much for your feedback. I'm one of the maintainers of VCC, and I'll save this item to discuss within our team.

David

---

**Issue URL:** https://github.com/visa/visa-chart-components/issues/69
**Status:** closed
**Comments:** 1

# Issue #67: Pie Chart: Value (%) label not being read by screenreaders

## Question

Pie Chart: Value (%) label not being read by screenreaders

Pie Chart: Value (%) label not being read by screenreaders

To replicate: 
-Turn on screenreader (voiceover/chrome/mac or NVDA/Chrome/Win11)
- Go to piechart example: https://visa.github.io/visa-chart-components/?path=/story/pie-chart--default#storybook-preview-wrapper
- Navigate to piechart slice
- Note that Value(%) value is not read out by screen readers

Thanks for sharing this great work!

## Answer

I can confirm this is happening on my end too. Seems like a bug, since the expected experience should include that percentage!

@dreadnl, There are some easy ways to resolve this in the meantime though, if you wanted! You can pre-calculate the percentages and pass them in as an additional property of each data point. like: { category: "United States", value: 147, percentage: "33%"} and then use the property accessibility = {…, elementDescriptionAccessor: "percentage" } to get the percentage to read out after the first two values. The property `elementDescriptionAccessor` of the `accessibility` prop is used to add any additional information to screen readers that is otherwise missing or unclear based on the input data alone.


Additional discussion:
- This is fixed in the most recent release, closing the issue. ...

---

**Issue URL:** https://github.com/visa/visa-chart-components/issues/67
**Status:** closed
**Labels:** bug, needs investigation
**Comments:** 2

metadata.issue_title: Pie Chart: Value (%) label not being read by screenreaders

content:
# Issue #64: Initial Build of Pacakges for Monorepo Seems to Burn Down MacBook Air M1 Base Model

## Question

Initial Build of Pacakges for Monorepo Seems to Burn Down MacBook Air M1 Base Model

When I attempt to build the packages initially for the monorepo with the `yarn dev --b` command, the computer becomes unresponsive. I'm wondering if this is specific to me, or if it does happen to others as well?! I know the note says that the initial build might take some time...

## Answer

Thanks for the comment on this @davidkutas. Can you try reducing the concurrency of the build process and let me know if that helps. In package.json change the build script to concurrency 2 or 4 instead of 8, which is what it is set to. 

I have not tried to build on an M1, but it does build on my old MacBook, if it struggles I reduce the concurrency to help the machine out a bit. 

Let us know if that helps.


Additional discussion:
- I changed the concurrency to 2, and the issue got resolved. Thank you....
- Sounds good, we will try to add something to our docs to note this work around in an upcoming release. ...

---

**Issue URL:** https://github.com/visa/visa-chart-components/issues/64
**Status:** closed
**Labels:** documentation, fixed-in-next-release
**Comments:** 4

metadata.issue_title: Initial Build of Pacakges for Monorepo Seems to Burn Down MacBook Air M1 Base Model

# Issue #63: npm i @visa/charts-react peer dependency issue (currently fixable with legacy-peer-deps flag)

## Question

npm i @visa/charts-react peer dependency issue (currently fixable with legacy-peer-deps flag)

If you want to install the `@visa/charts-react` library with the latest `create-react-app`, you get an upstream dependency conflict. I assume that  the `create-react-app` is using `react@18.2.0` while your library is using `react@16.7.0`.

Upon trying to install `charts-react` with the `legacy-peer-deps` flag the installation works perfectly.

## Answer

This was addressed by our most recent release, closing this out. 

---

**Issue URL:** https://github.com/visa/visa-chart-components/issues/63
**Status:** closed
**Comments:** 1

metadata.issue_title: npm i @visa/charts-react peer dependency issue (currently fixable with legacy-peer-deps flag)

# Issue #32: Need documentation for Annotation special features

## Question

Need documentation for Annotation special features

The annotations section of the utils readme is okay: https://github.com/visa/visa-chart-components/tree/master/packages/utils#annotations

But the util has a specific feature (that you can add/subtract using nested arrays) that is not documented anywhere. 
This example is powerful and custom to the annotation util wrapper that is built into VCC (Susie Lu's documentation will not teach this feature): `x: [["category1"],["category2]]`

(That example will calculate the pixel value of category2 and subtract it from the calculated pixel value of category1.)

Not only that, but the only explanation of the special features of the util are shown in comments in the example code snippet. It would be nice if there was a table that shows valid things that could be passed to each property (and which properties take precedence over others, such as `x:` versus `data:`).

In addition to this missing piece of documentation in the utils, the documentation in each component only points to d3-annotation by Susie Lu. It does not point to the utils doc (which has additional examples).
Bar chart: https://github.com/visa/visa-chart-components/tree/master/packages/bar-chart#annotation-props

## Answer

This issue was closed without comments.

---

**Issue URL:** https://github.com/visa/visa-chart-components/issues/32
**Status:** closed
**Labels:** documentation, enhancement, fixed-in-next-release
**Comments:** 0

metadata.issue_title: Need documentation for Annotation special features

# Issue #25: Broken link in readme files

## Question

Broken link in readme files

The [readme for barchart](https://github.com/visa/visa-chart-components/tree/master/packages/bar-chart) points to the color utility file using this link:
https://github.com/visa/visa-chart-components/blob/master/packages/utils/src/colors.ts

It should point either to this location in the util's readme:
https://github.com/visa/visa-chart-components/tree/master/packages/utils#colors

Or it should point to the file it intended to (one level deeper):
https://github.com/visa/visa-chart-components/blob/master/packages/utils/src/utils/colors.ts

## Answer

This issue was closed without comments.
